UNITED STATES PATENT APPLICATION
TITLE OF THE INVENTION
Systems and Methods for Non-Bypassable Continuation Control Under Declared Adversarial
Refinement
INVENTOR
Jacob Moses Bookbinder
Atlanta, Georgia, United States of America
APPLICATION TYPE
Non-Provisional Utility Patent Application
RELATED APPLICATIONS
This application claims priority to and incorporates by reference the following provisional patent
applications:
• Provisional Application No. 63/946,214, filed December 22, 2025
• Provisional Application No. 63/948,154, filed December 24, 2025
• Provisional Application No. 63/948,562, filed December 25, 2025
STATEMENT REGARDING FEDERALLY SPONSORED RESEARCH OR
DEVELOPMENT
Not applicable.INCORPORATION BY REFERENCE
None.
FIELD OF THE INVENTION
The present invention relates to procedural control of continuation in computational,
organizational, and decision systems, and more particularly to non-bypassable mechanisms that
determine whether a process may continue execution, deployment, publication, authorization,
promotion, activation, or actuation at defined continuation boundaries.
ABSTRACT
Systems, methods, and non-transitory computer-readable media are disclosed for controlling
continuation of a target process at defined continuation boundaries. A continuation control
engine is synchronously invoked prior to execution of downstream effects and evaluates
admissibility of an evaluation scope under explicitly declared refinement operations, including
adversarial or perturbative transformations. The system produces a terminal continuation
outcome selected from a finite categorical set and enforces continuation through a non-
bypassable control path such that continuation is procedurally prohibited unless permitted.
Refusal or obstruction records are generated and persisted when continuation is non-permitting.
Continuation control operates independently of mathematical representation or formalism and
fails closed under uncertainty.
